statement: Hooks come only from REFramework Lua + plugin DLL; no memory patching
DD2 patches rotate IL2CPP layouts; typed access needs version guards
REFramework Lua runs on render thread; I/O goes off-thread via native plugin
Never read, write, or stat DD2 save files
Never ship Capcom assets (textures, meshes, scripts, audio, decompiled code)
Never mutate DD2 entities, pawns, flags, inventory, world state
Do not reverse-engineer DD2's pawn network; read state via IL2CPP only
One person. Every architectural decision is priced in weekends
Assume Tailscale/ZeroTier tailnet or LAN. NAT traversal not our problem
No DBs, no disk state, no migrations. In-memory ring buffers only
Zero distribution is an acceptable floor
Mod never mutates DD2 state; overlay draws only
Mod has no read or write access to DD2 save files
All hooks go through REFramework; no external loaders

title: Smallest REFramework plugin that runs a UDP socket off-thread
why: Highest-leverage unknown. Everything in ADR-0002 is aspirational until this works

title: What IL2CPP surface exposes camera matrices and player position
why: Needed for ghost projection to screen space

title: Does REFramework render hook fire on a thread safe for overlay draws

claim: REFramework v1.5.x actively maintained for DD2 as of April 2026; Lua can require native DLLs via plugin ABI with env quirks (see issue #623)
evidence: REFramework docs + GitHub issue #623 + Nexus DD2 mod page

claim: DD2 patches slowing: 2.01 Sept 2024, Jan 2025, Jan 2026 then quiet. Offset-break risk correspondingly lower
evidence: SteamDB patch notes + Capcom official update page
